zh.Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Co: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托達特姆斯

zh.Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colo: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼達特姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson於1608年出生在英國。他於1644年與Mary在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德波斯姆斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1651年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德波斯姆斯。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼被列為surveyer and farmer。他去世於從1683年10月18日到1683年11月15日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼達特姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson于1608年出生在英国。他于1644年与Mary在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德波斯姆斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1651年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德波斯姆斯。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼被列为surveyer and farmer。他去世于从1683年10月18日到1683年11月15日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼達特姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Sisson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English female personal name Sis(se), Cisse, a pet form of Cecily (see Sisley ) + -son.
variant of Syson, a habitational name from Syston (Leicestershire), from a short form of an Old English personal name such as Sigehǣth or Sigefrith + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Sissons
meanings: variant of Sisson , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Providence Founded
historicEvents.description: In 1636 Providence is founded as a settlement for Rhode Island by Roger Williams

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson was born in 1608, in England. He married Mary in 1644, in Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America in 1651. His occupation is listed as surveyer and farmer in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. He died from 18 October 1683 to 15 November 1683, in Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson est né en 1608, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Mary en 1644, à Portsmouth, Rhode Island, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Portsmouth, Rhode Island, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1651. Sa profession de son indiquée est surveyer and farmer à Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé du 18 octobre 1683 au 15 novembre 1683, à Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson è nato il nel 1608 nel England. Lui sposato Mary nel 1644, nel Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America nel 1651. La sua occupazione è indicata come surveyer and farmer nel Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Morì da 18 ottobre 1683 a 15 novembre 1683, nel Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Co: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルダルトモウス
ja.Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Coloni: イギリス植民地アメリカロード イスランドポルトスモウス
ja.Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colo: イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーダルトモウス
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Sissonは1608年にイギリスで生まれた。1644年にイギリス植民地アメリカロード イスランドポルトスモウスでMaryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1651年にはイギリス植民地アメリカロード イスランドポルトスモウスに住んでいた。イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーでの職業はsurveyer and farmerと記録されている。彼はから1683年10月18日まで1683年11月15日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーダルトモウスで死去した。

ko.Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Co: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 더르트모우스
ko.Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Coloni: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 호드 이슬랜드 포츠모우스
ko.Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 더르트모우스
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson(은)는 1608년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1644년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 호드 이슬랜드 포츠모우스에서 Mary(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1651년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 호드 이슬랜드 포츠모우스에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이에서 surveyer and farmer이었다. 그는 1683년 10월 18일부터 1683년 11월 15일까지 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 더르트모우스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson nasceu em 1608, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mary em 1644, em Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America em 1651. Sua profissão aparece como surveyer and farmer em Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu de 18 de outubro de 1683 a 15 de novembro de 1683, em Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson родился в 1608 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary в 1644 г. в местности порцмоут, род исланд, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности порцмоут, род исланд, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1651 году.  Он работал по профессии surveyer and farmer в местности плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер с 18 октября 1683 по 15 ноября 1683 в местности дартмоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Sisson nació en 1608, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Mary en 1644, en Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America en 1651. Su ocupación registrada era surveyer and farmer en Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Murió desde 18 de octubre de 1683 hasta 15 de noviembre de 1683, en Dartmouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.
